novel_features.name: Revenue snapshot
novel_features.description: Point-in-time MRR, ARR, active subscriptions, trials, and revenue in your chosen currency, with deltas against your last snapshot.
novel_features.rationale: Persists each run to a local snapshots table and diffs against the prior row, producing a current-moment-plus-delta view no single dashboard screen composes.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent or operator asks 'where's revenue right now and how did it move since last check' in one call.

novel_features.name: MRR trend
novel_features.description: MRR over time with new / expansion / contraction / churn movement decomposition and week-over-week deltas.
novel_features.rationale: Joins the mrr and mrr_movement chart series locally into one movement table the charts endpoint returns only separately.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to explain why MRR moved, not just that it moved.

novel_features.name: Churn watch
novel_features.description: Which subscriptions flipped to billing-issue, grace, expired, or cancelled in a window, with per-subscription dollar exposure.
novel_features.rationale: Joins the churn chart against the locally synced subscriptions mirror — a cross-source join the dashboard can't compose.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when you need who churned and how much MRR they took, not just the churn rate line.

novel_features.name: Dunning alert
novel_features.description: Subscriptions still in grace or billing-issue state joined with their unpaid invoices, ranked by recoverable amount.
novel_features.rationale: Local join of subscription status against invoices surfaces the recoverable window before it becomes churn.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to act on failed billing while the revenue is still recoverable.

novel_features.name: Entitlement rollup
novel_features.description: Per-entitlement active-customer counts and product attachments, flagging customers whose entitlement state disagrees with their subscription state.
novel_features.rationale: Three-way local join of project entitlements, per-customer active entitlements, and subscription status that no single API call returns.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to catch silent entitlement desync (a dropped webhook, a refund that didn't cascade) across the whole customer base.

novel_features.name: Refund cascade
novel_features.description: Traces a subscription or purchase through its transactions, refund history, and resulting entitlement loss; --apply issues the refund.
novel_features.rationale: Walks subscription to transactions to refund to entitlement fallout from the local mirror, with an opt-in live refund action.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to understand or execute a refund and see exactly which entitlements the customer loses.

novel_features.name: Trial funnel
novel_features.description: New trials to conversion-to-paying funnel with stage-to-stage drop-off.
novel_features.rationale: Joins the trials_new and conversion_to_paying chart series into one funnel the charts endpoint returns only as separate series.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to see where trials leak before they convert.

novel_features.name: Webhook audit
novel_features.description: Lists configured webhook integrations grouped by destination host, flagging duplicate or stale destinations.
novel_features.rationale: Local grouping of the webhooks mirror by destination surfaces config drift the dashboard list view doesn't.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to confirm webhook delivery config before a dropped event silently desyncs entitlements.

narrative.headline: Every RevenueCat v2 endpoint, plus a local database, offline search, and revenue/churn/refund intelligence the dashboard charts can't compose.
narrative.value_prop: Drive the full RevenueCat Developer API v2 from the shell or an agent: customers, subscriptions, entitlements, products, offerings, purchases, invoices, and charts. On top of the typed endpoint surface it adds a local SQLite mirror and novel commands — revenue-snapshot with run-over-run diffs, mrr-trend with movement decomposition, churn-watch with dollar exposure, dunning-alert, entitlement-rollup, refund-cascade, and trial-funnel — that join synced data in ways no single API call returns.
narrative.auth_narrative: RevenueCat v2 uses a server-side Secret API key sent as 'Authorization: Bearer <key>'. Create one in the RevenueCat dashboard under Project Settings -> API Keys -> Secret API keys (a read-only key is enough for every read command). This is distinct from the public SDK keys. Set REVENUECAT_API_KEY and REVENUECAT_PROJECT_ID in your environment; run 'revenuecat-pp-cli doctor' to confirm both resolve against your project.

quickstart.comment: Health check — confirms the binary and config wiring without calling the API.
Pull the core entities into the local store so offline and join commands work.
Current MRR/ARR/actives/revenue with deltas vs your last snapshot.
Who churned this week and the dollar exposure, as agent-native JSON.

troubleshoots.symptom: doctor reports the API key is missing or invalid
troubleshoots.fix: Set REVENUECAT_API_KEY to a v2 Secret key (starts with sk_) from Project Settings -> API Keys; the public SDK keys will not work.

troubleshoots.symptom: Commands return a project-not-found or 404 error
troubleshoots.fix: Set REVENUECAT_PROJECT_ID to your project id, or pass --project <id>; every v2 endpoint is project-scoped.

troubleshoots.symptom: HTTP 429 rate-limit errors during sync or fan-out
troubleshoots.fix: RevenueCat rate-limits per domain; re-run sync with a smaller --resources set or let the built-in adaptive limiter back off.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when you need programmatic or agent-driven visibility into RevenueCat mobile subscription state: revenue and MRR/ARR snapshots, churn and dunning analysis, entitlement reconciliation, refund tracing, and webhook config audits. It is the mobile-rail companion to a Lemon Squeezy CLI for the web rail.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI for the in-app purchase flow itself; that is the RevenueCat mobile SDK, not the server API.
Do not use this CLI for browsing RevenueCat blog posts, docs, or State-of-Subscription reports; that is the marketing site, not the Developer API.
Do not use this CLI for v1-only operations that v2 does not yet cover; fall back to the v1 API for those.

recipes.title: Morning standup revenue line
recipes.explanation: One pasteable line of MRR, active subs, and the change since the last snapshot.

recipes.title: Recoverable failed billing
recipes.explanation: Subscriptions still in grace or billing-issue joined with unpaid invoices, ranked by what you can still recover.

recipes.title: Find entitlement desync
recipes.explanation: Surfaces customers whose entitlement state disagrees with their subscription state across the whole base.

recipes.title: Trace a refund's fallout
recipes.explanation: Shows the transactions, refund history, and entitlements a customer loses before you issue the refund.
